params.text:
Active listening — the SOLER approach

Listening is an active skill, not just staying quiet. A simple way to remember good listening posture is SOLER:

  • S — Sit or face the person squarely
  • O — Keep an open posture (don't fold your arms)
  • L — Lean slightly towards the person
  • E — Maintain comfortable eye contact
  • R — Stay relaxed

Add verbal signs you're listening — nodding, "I see", reflecting back what you heard — and give the person time. Never rush or finish their sentences.

params.alt: Animation of sound travelling from a speaker to an attentive listener

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap to complete these communication principles.
params.textField:
Communication is a *two-way* process between a sender and a receiver.
Around half of any message is carried through *non-verbal* signals such as body language.
To show you are listening, you should *reflect* back what the person has said.
Always *check* understanding rather than assuming the message was received.
Written records must be clear, factual and *timely*.
